problem_statement: Edit replay.py so normalize_tool_call(tool_call) returns an OpenAI-compatible assistant tool call shape. Preserve the id, set type to function, and nest the tool name and arguments under function. Verify the tests pass.

files.test_replay.py:
from replay import normalize_tool_call


def test_normalize_tool_call_openai_shape():
    payload = normalize_tool_call({'id': 'call_1', 'name': 'write', 'arguments': {'path': 'a.py'}})
    assert payload['id'] == 'call_1'
    assert payload['type'] == 'function'
    assert payload['function']['name'] == 'write'
    assert payload['function']['arguments'] == {'path': 'a.py'}

files.urls.py:
def normalize_anthropic_base_url(base_url: str | None) -> str | None:
    return base_url

problem_statement: Edit adapter_config.py so build_direct_config(real_api_base, real_api_key, real_model) chooses Anthropic credentials when the model starts with claude- and OpenAI credentials otherwise. Verify the tests pass.

files.test_adapter_config.py:
from adapter_config import build_direct_config


def test_claude_model_uses_anthropic_keys():
    config = build_direct_config('https://api.example.com/v1', 'key', 'claude-sonnet-4-6')
    assert config['model'] == 'claude-sonnet-4-6'
    assert config['anthropic_api_base'] == 'https://api.example.com/v1'
    assert config['anthropic_api_key'] == 'key'
    assert 'openai_api_base' not in config


def test_openai_model_uses_openai_keys():
    config = build_direct_config('https://api.example.com/v1', 'key', 'gpt-5.4')
    assert config['model'] == 'gpt-5.4'
    assert config['openai_api_base'] == 'https://api.example.com/v1'
    assert config['openai_api_key'] == 'key'
    assert 'anthropic_api_base' not in config

problem_statement: Edit parser.py so extract_choices(response) raises a clear RuntimeError when the provider returns a raw string or HTML instead of an OpenAI chat object. Verify the tests pass.
Edit trace_utils.py so build_tool_trace(name, arguments, content) returns a trace event payload with nested input/output fields matching the production trace shape. Verify the tests pass.

Edit router_logic.py so get_provider(model_id, preferred_provider, adapters, model_cache) always returns the preferred provider when it is enabled, even if the model cache points somewhere else. Verify the tests pass.

files.router_logic.py:
def get_provider(model_id: str, preferred_provider: str | None, adapters: dict, model_cache: dict) -> str | None:
    return model_cache.get(model_id)
files.test_router_logic.py:
from router_logic import get_provider


def test_preferred_provider_wins_when_enabled():
    provider = get_provider('minimax-m2.5', 'anthropic', {'anthropic': object(), 'openai': object()}, {'minimax-m2.5': 'openai'})
    assert provider == 'anthropic'


def test_cached_provider_used_without_preference():
    provider = get_provider('minimax-m2.5', None, {'anthropic': object(), 'openai': object()}, {'minimax-m2.5': 'openai'})
    assert provider == 'openai'

problem_statement: Edit timeout_trace.py so make_timeout_event(model, timeout_seconds, message_count) returns an error event payload named llm_timeout with all three fields preserved. Verify the tests pass.

files.test_timeout_trace.py:
from timeout_trace import make_timeout_event


def test_timeout_event_shape():
    event = make_timeout_event('claude-sonnet-4-6', 300.0, 2)
    assert event['name'] == 'llm_timeout'
    assert event['data']['model'] == 'claude-sonnet-4-6'
    assert event['data']['timeout_seconds'] == 300.0
    assert event['data']['message_count'] == 2

problem_statement: Edit responses_fallback.py so should_fallback(error_message) returns True when the upstream likely does not support /responses, but returns False for non-OpenAI string payload errors. Verify the tests pass.

files.responses_fallback.py:
def should_fallback(error_message: str) -> bool:
    return 'responses' in error_message.lower()
